content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

January 10, 1990


John L. Taylor, Clerk
Board of Supervisors
City and County of San Francisco
City Hall
San Francisco, CA 94102

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-90-748

Dear Mr. Taylor:

By letter dated December 11, 1990 you have requested advice
under the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform
Act (the "Act").¹ We are unable to provide you with the advice
you seek.

Your letter indicates that you have been directed by the
board to inquire why three supervisors - Ms. Alioto, Mr. Hallinan,
and Mr. Hongisto - "could not vote" on a proposed condominium
conversion ordinance (166-90-1) at the board's December 10, 1990
meeting. No indication is provided to suggest that the inquiry is
being made with the authorization, and therefore in a
representative capacity on behalf, of the three supervisors.

The Commission is authorized by statute to provide advice to
persons with questions about their own responsibilities under the Act.
We do not provide advice to third parties about another person's
duties or obligations unless the third party has been authorized
to request advice by the person whose duties or obligations are in
question. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329, copy enclosed.) For
this reason, we cannot provide you with the advice you have
requested.²


_________________________

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2 For your information, the Commission has received telephone
inquiries from the city attorney's office on matters similar or
related to this issue on behalf of Supervisors Alioto and
Hallinan. Supervisor Hongisto, and the city attorney on his
behalf, have also contacted us.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

File No. I-90-748
Page 2

     Additionally, if the Board's direction somehow constitutes
official authorization on behalf of the three supervisors named in
your letter, we are still unable to more fully respond to your
inquiry.  The letter contains insufficient facts to permit a
conflict of interest determination.  (Regulation 18329(b)(2)(B)
and (b)(8)(C).)

     I am sorry that we cannot provide you with the assistance you
requested.  If you have any further questions regarding this
matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Scott Hallabrin
                                        Acting General Counsel

By: Jonathan S. Rothman
     Counsel, Legal Division

SH:JSR:ken

Enclosures
